segments.text: When Jesus had finished giving instructions to his twelve disciples, he left to go and teach and speak publicly in the nearby towns.
While John was in prison he heard what the Messiah was doing, so he sent his disciples
to ask on his behalf, {
Are you the one we were expecting to come, or should we continue to look for someone else?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus answered them, {
Go back and tell John what you hear and what you see.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The blind can see, the crippled can walk, the lepers are healed, the deaf can hear, the dead are resurrected, and the poor hear the good news.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blessed are those who don't reject me!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As they left, Jesus began to talk to the crowds about John. {
What were you expecting to see when you went out into the desert? A reed tossed about by the wind?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So what did you go to see? A man dressed in fancy clothes? People dressed like that live in kings' palaces.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So what did you go to see then? A prophet? Yes, and I tell you he is much more than a prophet!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He's the one this Scripture was written about: ‘I'm sending my messenger on ahead of you. He will prepare your way for you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, that among humanity there's no one greater than John the Baptist, but even the least important in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he is.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: From the time of John the Baptist up till now the kingdom of heaven continues to be under attack, and violent people are trying to seize it by force.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For all the prophets and the law spoke for God until John came.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you're prepared to believe it, he is Elijah, the one who was expected to come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Anybody with ears should be listening!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What shall I compare this generation to? It's like children sitting in the marketplaces shouting to each other,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘We played the flute for you and you didn't dance; we sang sad songs and you didn't cry.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: John didn't come feasting and drinking, so people say, ‘He's devil-possessed!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: On the other hand the Son of man came and did feast and drink, and people say, ‘Look, he's greedy and drinks too much; he's a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is shown to be right by what it does…
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he began to reprimand the towns where he'd done most of his miracles because they had not repented.
Shame on you, Korazin! Shame on you, Bethsaida! If the miracles had been performed in Tyre and Sidon which happened among you, they would have repented in sackcloth and ashes long ago.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But I tell you that on the Day of Judgment it will be better for Tyre and Sidon than it will for you!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And what about you, Capernaum? Will you be exalted to heaven? No, you'll go down to Hades! If the miracles had been performed in Sodom which happened among you, Sodom would still be here today.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But I tell you that on the Day of Judgment it will be better for Sodom than it will for you!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus prayed, {
I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, for you've hidden these things from the clever and sophisticated. Instead you've revealed them to ordinary people.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yes, Father, you were pleased to do this!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Everything has been entrusted to me by my Father, and no one really understands the Son, except the Father, and no one really understands the Father, except the Son, and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Come to me, all of you who struggle and who are burdened down. I will give you rest.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Accept my yoke, and learn from me. For I am kind and have a humble heart, and in me you will find the rest you need.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For my yoke is gentle, and my burden is light.
segments.speaker: Jesus
